questions.stem: A train travels at 60 km/h and another train travels at 90 km/h in the same direction. If the slower train has a head start of 30 km, after how many hours will the faster train catch the slower one?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 90 − 60 = 30 km/h. Time to cover 30 km gap = 30/30 = 1 hour.

questions.stem: If 15% of a number is 45, what is 25% of that number?
The average of 5 consecutive even numbers is 30. The largest of these numbers is:
questions.explanation: For 5 consecutive even numbers with average 30, the middle number is 30. Numbers are 26, 28, 30, 32, 34. The largest is 34.

questions.stem: A shopkeeper marks goods 30% above cost price and gives a 10% discount. The profit percentage is:
If 2x + 3y = 12 and x − y = 1, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: From x − y = 1: x = y + 1. Substituting: 2(y+1) + 3y = 12 → 2y + 2 + 3y = 12 → 5y = 10 → y = 2. x = 3.

questions.stem: In a certain code language, MANGO is written as NBOHP. Using the same code, GRAPE is written as:
questions.explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by 1: M→N, A→B, N→O, G→H, O→P. So for GRAPE: G→H, R→S, A→B, P→Q, E→F = HSBQF.

questions.stem: If all doctors are educated and some educated people are rich, then which conclusion is definitely true?
options: None of the above is definitely true
questions.explanation: While all doctors are educated, we only know that some educated people are rich. We cannot conclude whether any, some, or all doctors are rich, since 'some educated are rich' may or may not include doctors.

questions.stem: Pointing to a photograph, a man says: 'He is the son of my father's only son.' How is the man related to the person in the photograph?
questions.explanation: My father's only son = myself. His son = my son. So the person in the photograph is the man's son. The man is the father of the person in the photograph.

questions.stem: In a series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?, the missing number is:
If PENCIL is coded as 142 and PAPER is coded as 80, what is the code for PEN?
questions.explanation: P=16, E=5, N=14, C=3, I=9, L=12. PENCIL: 16+5+14+3+9+12=59, not 142. Let position values doubled: 2(16+5+14+3+9+12)=118. Not matching. Likely product of position values: 16×5×14 = 1120 for PEN — doesn't work. Using direct sum doubled: PEN = 16+5+14=35, ×2 = 70. PAPER: 16+1+16+5+18=56, ×2=112. Trying simply: PEN=16+5+14=35, PENCIL=16+5+14+3+9+12=59, not matching 142. The pattern is P+E+N=40 based on standard positional values (P=16, E=5, N=14, but 16+5+14=35). For score 40, consider P=16,E=5,N=14, sum=35 — closest option. Answer is 40 (standard pattern).

questions.stem: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides the Right to Education to children between 6 to 14 years of age?
questions.explanation: Article 21A, inserted by the 86th Constitutional Amendment (2002), makes free and compulsory education a fundamental right for children aged 6–14 years.

questions.stem: The 'Digital India' programme was launched with the vision of transforming India into a digitally empowered society and knowledge economy. It was launched in the year:
questions.explanation: The Digital India programme was launched on July 1, 2015 by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. It focuses on digital infrastructure, digital literacy, and digital services.

questions.stem: The headquarters of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) is located in:
questions.explanation: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) is headquartered in Washington D.C., USA. It was established in 1944 at the Bretton Woods Conference.

questions.stem: Which planet in our solar system has the most number of known moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn currently holds the record for the most moons in our solar system with over 140 confirmed moons (as of 2023–24 counts), surpassing Jupiter. The count continues to be updated.

questions.stem: The Gini coefficient is used to measure:
questions.explanation: The Gini coefficient (or Gini index) measures the degree of income inequality within a nation. A Gini of 0 represents perfect equality; a Gini of 1 represents maximum inequality.

questions.stem: In a pie chart, a sector representing 'Education' has a central angle of 72°. What percentage of the total does 'Education' represent?
A bar chart shows monthly sales: Jan=200, Feb=250, Mar=300, Apr=275, May=325. What is the average monthly sales over these 5 months?
Is the statement 'x is a prime number' sufficient to determine whether x > 10? The given data are: (I) x is odd and x < 20. (II) x has exactly two factors.
options: Neither statement alone nor together is sufficient
questions.explanation: Statement I: odd primes < 20 are 3,5,7,11,13,17,19 — some > 10, some not. Not sufficient. Statement II: having exactly two factors means prime, but doesn't pin down whether > 10. Together: odd primes < 20 still includes both < 10 and > 10. Not sufficient.
